steps.title: Recall the standard circle equation
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ satisfies:
$$(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$$
The right-hand side is the radius squared.

steps.title: Substitute the given center and radius
Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$(x-16)^2+(y-17)^2=49k$': The right side is $49k$, not $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$. The radius was not properly squared. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(x-16)^2+(y-17)^2=49k^2$': $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$. Center and radius are applied correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — '$(x-16)^2+(y-17)^2=7k$': The right side is just $7k$, which would mean $r = \sqrt{7k}$, not $7k$. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(x-16)^2+(y-17)^2=7k^2$': $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$, not $7k^2$. Only $k$ was squared, not the full radius. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
